paragraphs.html: Open wide your minds and see, my brethren — the secret column in the air, whose base is fixed from the midst of the water — unto the door of the Highest Place, like the ladder that Jacob saw.— Lo! By it came down the light unto Baptism, — and by it the soul goes up to Heaven — that in one love we may be mingled.

paragraphs.text: Our Lord when he was baptized by John — sent forth twelve fountains — and they issued forth and cleansed by their streams — the defilement of the peoples.— His worshippers are made white like His garments — the garments in Tabor and the body in the water.— Instead of the garments the peoples are made white — and have become for Him a clothing of glory.

From your garments learn, my brethren — how your members should be kept.— For if the garment, which ever so many times — may be made clean — is duly kept for the sake of its comeliness — the body which has but one baptism— manifold more exceeding is the care of its keeping — for manifold are its dangers.
paragraphs.html: From your garments learn, my brethren — how your members should be kept.— For if the garment, which ever so many times — may be made clean — is duly kept for the sake of its comeliness — the body which has but one baptism— manifold more exceeding is the care of its keeping — for manifold are its dangers.

paragraphs.text: Again the sun in a house that is strait — is straitened therein though he be great:— but in a house that is goodly and large — when he rises thereon — far and wide in it he spreads his rays — and though the sun is one and the same in his nature, — in various houses he undergoes changes:— Even so our Lord in various men.
paragraphs.html: Again the sun in a house that is strait — is straitened therein though he be great:— but in a house that is goodly and large — when he rises thereon — far and wide in it he spreads his rays — and though the sun is one and the same in his nature, — in various houses he undergoes changes:— Even so our Lord in various men.

paragraphs.text: (Resp.— Glory to Him Who came and restored it!)
paragraphs.html: (Resp.— Glory to Him Who came and restored it!)

paragraphs.text: Adam sinned and earned all sorrows — likewise the world after His example, all guilt.— And instead of considering how it should be restored — considered how its fall should be pleasant for it.— Glory to Him Who came and restored it!
paragraphs.html: Adam sinned and earned all sorrows — likewise the world after His example, all guilt.— And instead of considering how it should be restored — considered how its fall should be pleasant for it.— Glory to Him Who came and restored it!

paragraphs.text: This cause summoned Him that is pure — that He should come and be baptized, even He with the defiled —Heaven for His glory was rent asunder.— That the purifier of all might be baptized with all — He came down and sanctified the water for our baptism.

For that cause for which He entered into the womb — for the same cause He went down into the river.— For that cause for which He entered into the grave — for the same cause He makes us enter into His chamber.— He perfected mankind for every cause.
paragraphs.html: For that cause for which He entered into the womb — for the same cause He went down into the river.— For that cause for which He entered into the grave — for the same cause He makes us enter into His chamber.— He perfected mankind for every cause.

paragraphs.text: His Conception is the store of our blessings;— His Birth is the treasury of our joys;— His Baptism is the cause of our pardon — His Death is the cause of our life.— Death He alone has overcome in His Resurrection.

At His Birth a star of light shone in the air — when He was baptized light flashed from the water — at His Death the sun was darkened in the firmament;— at His Passion the luminaries set along with Him — at His Epiphany the luminaries arose with Him.

Revealed was His Glory because of His Majesty;— revealed was His Passion because of His Manhood;— revealed was His Love because of His Graciousness;— revealed was His Judgment because of His Justice.— He has poured forth His attributes, on them that were His.

That whoever has looked on His Glory and despised Him — may look again on His Glory and worship Him — and whoever has scorned to taste of His Graciousness — may fear lest he be made to feel His justice — He has poured forth His helps on His worshippers.

Lo! The East in the morning was made light!— lo! The South at noonday was made dark!— The West again in turn at eventide was made light.— The three quarters represent the one Birth;— His Death and His Life they declare.

His Birth flowed on and was joined to His Baptism;— and His Baptism again flowed on even to His Death — His Death led and reached to His Resurrection, — a fourfold bridge unto His Kingdom; and lo! His sheep pass over in His footsteps.

And like as, save by the door of birth — none can enter into creation;— so, save by the door of resurrection, — none can enter into the Kingdom — and whoever has cut off his bridge, has brought to nought his hope.

He put on His armour and conquered and was crowned — He left His armour on earth and ascended, — that if any man desires the crown — he may resort to the armour and win by it — the crown of victory which he yearns after.

He fulfilled righteousness on earth, and ascended. — But if He, the All-cleanser, was baptized — What man is there that shall not be baptized?— for grace has come to baptism— to wash away the foulness of our wound.

The compulsion of God is an all-prevailing force — [but that is not pleasing to Him which is of compulsion,] — as that which is of discerning will. — Therefore in our fruits He calls us — who live not as under compulsion, by persuasion.
paragraphs.html: The compulsion of God is an all-prevailing force — [but that is not pleasing to Him which is of compulsion,] — as that which is of discerning will. — Therefore in our fruits He calls us — who live not as under compulsion, by persuasion.

paragraphs.text: Good is He, for lo! He labours in these two things — He wills not to constrain our freedom — nor again does He suffer us to abuse it.— For had he constrained it, He had taken away its power — and had He let it go, He had deprived it of help.

He knows that if He constrains He deprives us — He knows that if He casts off He destroys us — He knows that if He teaches He wins us.— He has not constrained and He has not cast off, as the Evil One does:— He has taught, chastened, and won us, as being the good God.
paragraphs.html: He knows that if He constrains He deprives us — He knows that if He casts off He destroys us — He knows that if He teaches He wins us.— He has not constrained and He has not cast off, as the Evil One does:— He has taught, chastened, and won us, as being the good God.

paragraphs.text: He knows that His treasuries abound:— the keys of His treasuries He has put into our hands.— He has made the Cross our treasurer — to open for us the gates of Paradise, — as Adam opened the gate of Gehenna.
(Resp.— Let the bodies rejoice which the Evil One had made naked, that in the water they have put on their glory!)
paragraphs.html: (Resp.— Let the bodies rejoice which the Evil One had made naked, that in the water they have put on their glory!)

paragraphs.text: Give thanks, O daughter, that your crownings have been doubled — for lo! Your temples and your sons rejoice.— The dedication of your temples is in the ministration — The dedication of your sons is in the anointing. — Blessed are you that at once......—......the tabernacle for them that dwell in you — and the Spirit has abode upon your sons!
paragraphs.html: Give thanks, O daughter, that your crownings have been doubled — for lo! Your temples and your sons rejoice.— The dedication of your temples is in the ministration — The dedication of your sons is in the anointing. — Blessed are you that at once......— ......the tabernacle for them that dwell in you — and the Spirit has abode upon your sons!

paragraphs.text: Our Lord opened up Baptism — in the midst of Jordan the blessed river.— The height and the depth rejoiced in Him — He brings forth the first fruits of His peace from the water — for they are first fruits, the fruits of Baptism. — The good God in His compassion will bring to pass — that His peace shall be first fruits on earth.
paragraphs.html: Our Lord opened up Baptism — in the midst of Jordan the blessed river.— The height and the depth rejoiced in Him — He brings forth the first fruits of His peace from the water — for they are first fruits, the fruits of Baptism. — The good God in His compassion will bring to pass — that His peace shall be first fruits on earth.

paragraphs.text: Moses stretched out the temporal Tabernacle;— the priests bathed themselves in water — and went in and ministered; and were stricken and punished — because their heart within was not cleansed.— Blessed are you that in the Passover of the great Passion, — the priests by the savour of their oblations, — lo! Are cleansing souls in you!

Great was the mystery that the Prophet saw — the torrent that was mighty.— Into its depths he gazed and beheld — your beauty instead of himself; you it was he saw, for your faith passes not away — you whose flood unseen shall overwhelm — the subtleties of idolatry.

Though John was great among them that are born of women — yet he that is little is greater than he — in this that his baptized were again baptized — in the baptism that was of the Apostles. — Blessed art you that your priest is greater than he — in this alone that forever — abides his baptism.
paragraphs.html: Though John was great among them that are born of women — yet he that is little is greater than he — in this that his baptized were again baptized — in the baptism that was of the Apostles. — Blessed art you that your priest is greater than he — in this alone that forever — abides his baptism.

paragraphs.text: The baptism that was of Siloam — did not bring mercy to the man that was laid there— who for thirty and eight years awaited it — for he was a respecter of the persons of the Levites.— Blessed art you that your healing is in you for all men — and your priests are devoted and ready — for all that are in need of your help.
paragraphs.html: The baptism that was of Siloam — did not bring mercy to the man that was laid there— who for thirty and eight years awaited it — for he was a respecter of the persons of the Levites.— Blessed art you that your healing is in you for all men — and your priests are devoted and ready — for all that are in need of your help.

paragraphs.text: The Prophet healed the waters that were unwholesome — and cured the disease of the land that was barren — so that its death was done away and its region resounded, for its offspring increased and its bosom was filled.— Greater is Your grace, Lord, than Elisha's!— Multiply my lambs and my flocks — at the great stream of my fountain! Ezekiel 47:1, sq
paragraphs.html: The Prophet healed the waters that were unwholesome — and cured the disease of the land that was barren — so that its death was done away and its region resounded, for its offspring increased and its bosom was filled.— Greater is Your grace, Lord, than Elisha's!— Multiply my lambs and my flocks — at the great stream of my fountain! Ezekiel 47:1, sq

paragraphs.text: Great is the marvel that is within your abode — the flocks together with the Shepherds — those at the stream of the waters — two unseen with one manifest who baptizes. — Blessed is he who is baptized in their fountains!— for three arms have upheld him — and three Names have preserved him!
paragraphs.html: Great is the marvel that is within your abode — the flocks together with the Shepherds — those at the stream of the waters — two unseen with one manifest who baptizes. — Blessed is he who is baptized in their fountains!— for three arms have upheld him — and three Names have preserved him!

paragraphs.text: (Resp.— Blessed is He Who went down and was baptized in Jordan, and turned back the People from error!)
paragraphs.html: (Resp.— Blessed is He Who went down and was baptized in Jordan, and turned back the People from error!)

paragraphs.text: In Baptism Adam found again— that glory that was among the trees of Eden.— He went down, and received it out of the water — he put it on, and went up and was adorned therein.— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!
paragraphs.html: In Baptism Adam found again— that glory that was among the trees of Eden.— He went down, and received it out of the water — he put it on, and went up and was adorned therein.— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!

paragraphs.text: Man fell in the midst of Paradise, — and in baptism compassion restored him:— he lost his comeliness through Satan's envy — and found it again by God's grace.— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!
paragraphs.html: Man fell in the midst of Paradise, — and in baptism compassion restored him:— he lost his comeliness through Satan's envy — and found it again by God's grace.— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!

paragraphs.text: The wedded pair were adorned in Eden — but the serpent stole their crowns:— yet mercy crushed down the accursed one — and made the wedded pair goodly in their raiment.— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!

They clothed themselves with leaves of necessity;— but the Merciful had pity on their beauty — and instead of leaves of trees — He clothed them with glory in the water.— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!

Baptism is the well-spring of life — which the Son of God opened by His Life — and from His Side it has brought forth streams.— Come, all that thirst, come, rejoice!— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!

The Father has sealed Baptism, to exalt it — and the Son has espoused it to glorify it — and the Spirit with threefold seal — has stamped it, and it has shone in holiness.— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!
paragraphs.html: The Father has sealed Baptism, to exalt it — and the Son has espoused it to glorify it — and the Spirit with threefold seal — has stamped it, and it has shone in holiness.— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!

paragraphs.text: The Trinity that is unsearchable — has laid up treasures in baptism.— Descend, you poor, to its fountain!— and be enriched from it, you needy!— Blessed be He that has mercy on all!
Hymn of the Baptized.
(Resp.— Brethren, sing praises, to the Son of the Lord of all; Who has bound for you crowns, such as kings long for!)
paragraphs.html: (Resp .— Brethren, sing praises, to the Son of the Lord of all; Who has bound for you crowns, such as kings long for!)

paragraphs.text: Your garments glisten, my brethren, as snow — and fair is your shining in the likeness of Angels!
In the likeness of Angels, you have come up, beloved — from Jordan's river, in the armour of the Holy Ghost.
The bridal chamber that fails not, my brethren, you have received:— and the glory of Adam's house today you have put on.
The judgment that came of the fruit, was Adam's condemnation:— but for you victory, has arisen this day.
paragraphs.html: The judgment that came of the fruit, was Adam's condemnation:— but for you victory, has arisen this day.

paragraphs.text: Your vesture is shining, and goodly your crowns:— which the Firstborn has bound for you, by the priest's hand this day.
Woe in Paradise, did Adam receive:— but you have received, glory this day.
The armour of victory, you put on, my beloved:— in the hour when the priest, invoked the Holy Ghost.
The Angels rejoice, men here below exult:— in your feast, my brethren, wherein is no foulness.
The good things of Heaven, my brethren, you have received:— beware of the Evil One, lest he despoil you.
The day when He dawned, the Heavenly King:— opens for you His door, and bids you enter Eden.
Crowns that fade not away, are set on your heads:— hymns of praise hourly, let your mouths sing.
Adam by means of the fruit, God cast forth in sorrow:— but you He makes glad, in the bride-chamber of joy.
paragraphs.html: Adam by means of the fruit, God cast forth in sorrow:— but you He makes glad, in the bride-chamber of joy.

paragraphs.text: Who would not rejoice, in your bridechamber, my brethren? — for the Father with His Son, and the Spirit rejoice in you.
Unto you shall the Father, be a wall of strength:— and the Son a Redeemer, and the Spirit a guard.
Martyrs by their blood, glorify their crowns:— but you our Redeemer, by His Blood glorifies.
Watchers and Angels, joy over the repentant:— they shall joy over you my brethren, that unto them you are made like.
The fruit which Adam, tasted not in Paradise:— this day in your mouths, has been placed with joy.
Our Redeemer figured, His Body by the tree:— whereof Adam tasted not, because he had sinned.
The Evil One made war, and subdued Adam's house:— through your baptism, my brethren, lo! He is subdued this day.
Great is the victory, but today you have won:— if so be ye neglect not, you shall not perish, my brethren.
Glory to them that are robed, glory to Adam's house!— in the birth that is from the water, let them rejoice and be blessed!
paragraphs.html: Glory to them that are robed, glory to Adam's house!— in the birth that is from the water, let them rejoice and be blessed!

paragraphs.text: Praise to Him Who has robed, His Churches in glory!— glory to Him Who has magnified, the race of Adam's house.
Hymn concerning our Lord and John.
(Resp.— Glory to You, my Lord, for You — with joy Heaven and earth worship!)
paragraphs.html: (Resp .— Glory to You, my Lord, for You — with joy Heaven and earth worship!)

paragraphs.text: My thought bore me to Jordan, — and I saw a marvel when there was revealed — the glorious Bridegroom who to the Bride — shall bring freedom and holiness.
I saw John filled with wonder — and the multitudes standing about him — and the glorious Bridegroom bowed down — to the Son of the barren that he might baptize Him.
At the Word and the Voice my thought marvelled:— for lo! John was the Voice;— our Lord was manifested as the Word, that what was hidden should become revealed.
The Bride was espoused but knew not — who was the Bridegroom on whom she gazed:— the guests were assembled, the desert was filled — and our Lord was hidden among them.
Then the Bridegroom revealed Himself — and to John at the voice He drew near:— and the Forerunner was moved and said of Him — This is the Bridegroom Whom I proclaimed.
paragraphs.html: Then the Bridegroom revealed Himself — and to John at the voice He drew near:— and the Forerunner was moved and said of Him — <q>This is the Bridegroom Whom I proclaimed.</q>

paragraphs.text: He came to baptism Who baptizes all — and He showed Himself at Jordan. — John saw Him and drew back — deprecating, and thus he spoke:—
How, my Lord, do You will to be baptized — You Who in Your baptism atones all?— Baptism looks unto You — do You shed on it holiness and perfection?
paragraphs.html: <q>How, my Lord, do You will to be baptized — You Who in Your baptism atones all?— Baptism looks unto You — do You shed on it holiness and perfection?</q>

paragraphs.text: Our Lord said I will it so — draw near, baptize Me that My Will may be done.— Resist My Will you can not:— I shall be baptized of you, for thus I will it.
paragraphs.html: Our Lord said I will it so — draw near, baptize Me that My Will may be done.— Resist My Will you can not:— I shall be baptized of you, for thus I will it.

paragraphs.text: I entreat, my Lord, that I be not compelled — for this is hard that You have said to me —'I need you to baptize Me;'— for it is You that with Your hyssop purifies all.
paragraphs.html: <q>I entreat, my Lord, that I be not compelled — for this is hard that You have said to me —'I need you to baptize Me;'— for it is You that with Your hyssop purifies all.</q>

paragraphs.text: I have asked it, and it pleases Me that thus it should be — and you, John, why do you gainsay?— Allow righteousness to be fulfilled — and come, baptize Me; why are you standing there?
paragraphs.html: <q>I have asked it, and it pleases Me that thus it should be — and you, John, why do you gainsay?— Allow righteousness to be fulfilled — and come, baptize Me; why are you standing there?</q>

paragraphs.text: How can one openly grasp — in his hands the fire that burns?— O You that are fire have mercy on me — and bid me not come near You, for it is hard for me!
paragraphs.html: How can one openly grasp — in his hands the fire that burns?— O You that are fire have mercy on me — and bid me not come near You, for it is hard for me!

paragraphs.text: I have revealed to You My Will; what do you question?— Draw near, baptize Me, and you shall not be burned.— The bridechamber is ready; keep Me not back — from the wedding-feast that has been made ready.
paragraphs.html: <q>I have revealed to You My Will; what do you question?— Draw near, baptize Me, and you shall not be burned.— The bridechamber is ready; keep Me not back — from the wedding-feast that has been made ready.</q>

paragraphs.text: The Watchers fear and dare not — gaze on You lest they be blinded — and I, how, O my Lord, shall I baptize You?— I am too weak to draw near; blame me not!
paragraphs.html: <q>The Watchers fear and dare not — gaze on You lest they be blinded — and I, how, O my Lord, shall I baptize You?— I am too weak to draw near; blame me not!</q>

paragraphs.text: You fear; therefore gainsay not — against My Will in what I desire:— and Baptism has respect unto Me.— Accomplish the work to which you have been called!
paragraphs.html: <q>You fear; therefore gainsay not — against My Will in what I desire:— and Baptism has respect unto Me.— Accomplish the work to which you have been called!</q>

paragraphs.text: Lo! I proclaimed You at Jordan — in the ears of the people that believed not and if they shall see You baptized of me — they will doubt that You are the Lord.
paragraphs.html: <q>Lo! I proclaimed You at Jordan — in the ears of the people that believed not and if they shall see You baptized of me — they will doubt that You are the Lord.</q>

paragraphs.text: Lo! I am to be baptized in their sight — and the Father Who sent Me bears witness of Me — that I am His Son and in Me He is well pleased — to reconcile Adam who was under His wrath.
paragraphs.html: Lo! I am to be baptized in their sight — and the Father Who sent Me bears witness of Me — that I am His Son and in Me He is well pleased — to reconcile Adam who was under His wrath.

paragraphs.text: It becomes, me, O my Lord, to know my nature— that I am moulded out of the ground — and You the moulder Who forms all things:— I, then, why should I baptize You in water?
paragraphs.html: <q>It becomes, me, O my Lord, to know my nature— that I am moulded out of the ground — and You the moulder Who forms all things:— I, then, why should I baptize You in water?</q>

paragraphs.text: It becomes you to know why I have come — and for what cause I have desired that you should baptize Me.— It is the middle of the way wherein I have walked — withhold not Baptism.
paragraphs.html: <q>It becomes you to know why I have come — and for what cause I have desired that you should baptize Me.— It is the middle of the way wherein I have walked — withhold not Baptism.</q>

paragraphs.text: Small is the river whereto You have come — that You should lodge therein and it should cleanse You.— The heavens suffice not for Your mightiness;— how much less shall Baptism contain You!
paragraphs.html: <q>Small is the river whereto You have come — that You should lodge therein and it should cleanse You.— The heavens suffice not for Your mightiness;— how much less shall Baptism contain You!</q>

paragraphs.text: The womb is smaller than Jordan;— yet was I willing to lodge in the Virgin: — and as I was born from woman — so too am I to be baptized in Jordan.
paragraphs.html: The womb is smaller than Jordan;— yet was I willing to lodge in the Virgin: — and as I was born from woman — so too am I to be baptized in Jordan.

paragraphs.text: Lo! The hosts are standing!— the ranks of Watchers, lo! They worship! — And if I draw near, my Lord, to baptize You — I tremble for myself with quaking.
paragraphs.html: <q>Lo! The hosts are standing!— the ranks of Watchers, lo! They worship! — And if I draw near, my Lord, to baptize You — I tremble for myself with quaking.</q>

paragraphs.text: The hosts and multitudes call you happy — all of them, for that you baptize Me.— For this I have chosen you from the womb:— fear not, for I have willed it.
paragraphs.html: The hosts and multitudes call you happy — all of them, for that you baptize Me.— For this I have chosen you from the womb:— fear not, for I have willed it.

paragraphs.text: I have prepared the way as I was sent:— I have betrothed the Bride as I was commanded.— May Your Epiphany be spread over the world — now that You have come, and let me not baptize You!
paragraphs.html: <q>I have prepared the way as I was sent:— I have betrothed the Bride as I was commanded.— May Your Epiphany be spread over the world — now that You have come, and let me not baptize You!</q>

paragraphs.text: This is My preparation, for so have I willed;— I will go down and be baptized in Jordan, — and make bright the armour for them that are baptized — that they may be white in Me and I not be conquered.
paragraphs.html: <q>This is My preparation, for so have I willed;— I will go down and be baptized in Jordan, — and make bright the armour for them that are baptized — that they may be white in Me and I not be conquered.</q>

paragraphs.text: Son of the Father, why should I baptize You?— for lo! You are in Your Father and Your Father in You.— Holiness unto the priests You give — why do You ask for water that is common?
paragraphs.html: <q>Son of the Father, why should I baptize You?— for lo! You are in Your Father and Your Father in You.— Holiness unto the priests You give — why do You ask for water that is common?</q>

paragraphs.text: The children of Adam look unto Me — that I should work for them the new birth.— A way in the waters I will search out for them — and if I be not baptized this cannot be.
paragraphs.html: The children of Adam look unto Me — that I should work for them the new birth.— A way in the waters I will search out for them — and if I be not baptized this cannot be.

paragraphs.text: Pontiffs of You are consecrated —priests by Your hyssop are purified — the anointed and the kings You make.— Baptism, how shall it profit You?
paragraphs.html: Pontiffs of You are consecrated —priests by Your hyssop are purified — the anointed and the kings You make.— Baptism, how shall it profit You?

paragraphs.text: The Bride you betrothed to Me awaits Me — that I should go down, be baptized, and sanctify her.— Friend of the Bridegroom withhold Me not — from the washing that awaits Me.
paragraphs.html: <q>The Bride you betrothed to Me awaits Me — that I should go down, be baptized, and sanctify her.— Friend of the Bridegroom withhold Me not — from the washing that awaits Me.</q>

paragraphs.text: I am not able, for I am weak — Your blaze in my hands to grasp.— Lo! Your legions are as flame — bid one of the Watchers baptize You!
paragraphs.html: <q>I am not able, for I am weak — Your blaze in my hands to grasp.— Lo! Your legions are as flame — bid one of the Watchers baptize You!</q>

paragraphs.text: Not from the Watchers was My Body assumed — that I should summon a Watcher to baptize Me.— The body of Adam, lo! I have put on — and you, son of Adam, art to baptize Me.
paragraphs.html: <q>Not from the Watchers was My Body assumed — that I should summon a Watcher to baptize Me.— The body of Adam, lo! I have put on — and you, son of Adam, art to baptize Me.</q>

paragraphs.text: The waters saw You, and greatly feared;— the waters saw You, and lo! They tremble!— The river foams in its terror — and I that am weak, how shall I baptize You?
paragraphs.html: The waters saw You, and greatly feared;— the waters saw You, and lo! They tremble!— The river foams in its terror — and I that am weak, how shall I baptize You?

paragraphs.text: The waters in My Baptism are sanctified, — and fire and the Spirit from Me shall they receive — and if I be not baptized they are not made perfect — to be fruitful of children that shall not die.
paragraphs.html: <q>The waters in My Baptism are sanctified, — and fire and the Spirit from Me shall they receive — and if I be not baptized they are not made perfect — to be fruitful of children that shall not die.</q>

paragraphs.text: Fire, if to Your fire it draw near — shall be burnt up of it as stubble.— The mountains of Sinai endured You not — and I that am weak, wherein shall I baptize You?
paragraphs.html: Fire, if to Your fire it draw near — shall be burnt up of it as stubble.— The mountains of Sinai endured You not — and I that am weak, wherein shall I baptize You?

paragraphs.text: I am the flaming fire — yet for man's sake I became a babe — in the virgin womb of the maiden. — And now I am to be baptized in Jordan.
paragraphs.html: <q>I am the flaming fire — yet for man's sake I became a babe — in the virgin womb of the maiden. — And now I am to be baptized in Jordan.</q>

paragraphs.text: It is very meet that You should baptize me — for You have holiness to purify all.— In You it is that the defiled are made holy; but You that art holy, why are You to be baptized?
paragraphs.html: It is very meet that You should baptize me — for You have holiness to purify all.— In You it is that the defiled are made holy; but You that art holy, why are You to be baptized?

paragraphs.text: It is very right that you should baptize Me — as I bid, and should not gainsay.— Lo! I baptized you within the womb —baptize me in Jordan!
paragraphs.html: <q>It is very right that you should baptize Me — as I bid, and should not gainsay.— Lo! I baptized you within the womb —baptize me in Jordan!</q>

paragraphs.text: I am a bondman and I am weak.— You that frees all have mercy on me! Your latchets to unloose I am not able — Your exalted head who will make me worthy to touch?
paragraphs.html: I am a bondman and I am weak.— You that frees all have mercy on me! Your latchets to unloose I am not able — Your exalted head who will make me worthy to touch?

paragraphs.text: Bondmen in My Baptism are set free — handwritings in My washing are blotted out — manumissions in the water are sealed;— and if I be not baptized all these come to nought.
paragraphs.html: <q>Bondmen in My Baptism are set free — handwritings in My washing are blotted out — manumissions in the water are sealed;— and if I be not baptized all these come to nought.</q>

paragraphs.text: A mantle of fire the air wears — and waits for You, above Jordan;— and if You consent to it and will to be baptized — You shall baptize Yourself and fulfil all.
paragraphs.html: <q>A mantle of fire the air wears — and waits for You, above Jordan;— and if You consent to it and will to be baptized — You shall baptize Yourself and fulfil all.</q>

paragraphs.text: This is meet, that you should baptize Me — that none may err and say concerning Me —'Had He not been alien from the Father's house — why feared the Levite to baptize Him?'
paragraphs.html: <q>This is meet, that you should baptize Me — that none may err and say concerning Me —'Had He not been alien from the Father's house — why feared the Levite to baptize Him?'</q>

paragraphs.text: The prayer, then, when You are baptized — how shall I complete over Jordan? — When the Father and the Spirit are seen over You — Whom shall I call on, as priest?
paragraphs.html: <q>The prayer, then, when You are baptized — how shall I complete over Jordan? — When the Father and the Spirit are seen over You — Whom shall I call on, as priest?</q>

paragraphs.text: The prayer in silence is to be completed:— come, your hand alone lay on Me.— and the Father shall utter in the priest's stead — that which is meet concerning His Son.
paragraphs.html: <q>The prayer in silence is to be completed:— come, your hand alone lay on Me.— and the Father shall utter in the priest's stead — that which is meet concerning His Son.</q>

paragraphs.text: They that are bidden, lo! All of them stand — the Bridegroom's guests, lo! They bear witness— that day by day I said among them —'I am the Voice and not the Word.'
paragraphs.html: <q>They that are bidden, lo! All of them stand — the Bridegroom's guests, lo! They bear witness— that day by day I said among them —'I am the Voice and not the Word.'</q>

paragraphs.text: Voice of him that cries in the wilderness — fulfil the work for which you came — that the desert whereunto you went out may resound — with the mighty peace you preached therein.
paragraphs.html: <q>Voice of him that cries in the wilderness — fulfil the work for which you came — that the desert whereunto you went out may resound — with the mighty peace you preached therein.</q>

paragraphs.text: The shout of the Watchers has come to my ears — lo! I hear from the Father's house — the hosts that sound forth the cry —'In Your Epiphany, O Bridegroom, the worlds have life.'
paragraphs.html: <q>The shout of the Watchers has come to my ears — lo! I hear from the Father's house — the hosts that sound forth the cry —'In Your Epiphany, O Bridegroom, the worlds have life.'</q>

paragraphs.text: The time hastes on, and the marriage guests — look to Me to see what is doing.— Come, baptize Me, that they may give praise — to the Voice of the Father when it is heard!
paragraphs.html: <q>The time hastes on, and the marriage guests — look to Me to see what is doing.— Come, baptize Me, that they may give praise — to the Voice of the Father when it is heard!</q>

paragraphs.text: I hearken, my Lord, according to Your Word: — come to Baptism as Your love constrains You!— The dust worships that whereunto he has attained — that on Him Who fashioned him he should lay his hand.
paragraphs.html: <q>I hearken, my Lord, according to Your Word: — come to Baptism as Your love constrains You!— The dust worships that whereunto he has attained — that on Him Who fashioned him he should lay his hand.</q>

paragraphs.text: The heavenly ranks were silent as they stood — and the Bridegroom went down into Jordan;— the Holy One was baptized and straightway went up — and His Light shone forth on the world.
paragraphs.html: The heavenly ranks were silent as they stood — and the Bridegroom went down into Jordan;— the Holy One was baptized and straightway went up — and His Light shone forth on the world.

paragraphs.text: The doors of the highest were opened above — and the voice of the Father was heard —This is my Beloved in Whom I am well pleased.— All you peoples, come and worship Him.
paragraphs.html: The doors of the highest were opened above — and the voice of the Father was heard —<q>This is my Beloved in Whom I am well pleased.</q>— All you peoples, come and worship Him.

paragraphs.text: They that saw were amazed as they stood, at the Spirit Who came down and bore witness to Him.— Praise to Your Epiphany that gladdens all — You in Whose revelation the worlds are lightened!
In the Birth of the Son light dawned, — and darkness fled from the world — and the earth was enlightened; then let it give glory— to the brightness of the Father Who has enlightened it!

He dawned from the womb of the Virgin, — and the shadows passed away when He was seen — and the darkness of error was strangled by Him — and the ends of the earth were enlightened that they should give glory.
Among the peoples there was great tumult — and in the darkness the light dawned, — and the nations rejoiced to give glory— to Him in Whose Birth they all were enlightened.
His light shone out over the east —Persia was enlightened by the star:— His Epiphany gave good tidings to her and invited her —He has come for the sacrifice that brings joy to all.
paragraphs.html: His light shone out over the east —Persia was enlightened by the star:— His Epiphany gave good tidings to her and invited her —He has come for the sacrifice that brings joy to all.

paragraphs.text: The star of light hasted and came and dawned — through the darkness, and summoned them — that the peoples should come and exult — in the great Light that has come down to earth.
One envoy from among the stars — the firmament sent to proclaim to them — to the sons of Persia, that they might make ready — to meet the King and to worship Him.
Great Assyria when she perceived it— called to the Magi and said to them —Take gifts and go, honour Him — the great King Who in Judea has dawned.
paragraphs.html: Great Assyria when she perceived it— called to the Magi and said to them —Take gifts and go, honour Him — the great King Who in Judea has dawned.

paragraphs.text: The princes of Persia, exulting — carried gifts from their region — and they brought to the Son of the Virgin — gold and myrrh and frankincense.
They entered and found Him as a child — as He dwelt in the house of the lowly woman — and they drew near and worshipped with gladness — and brought near before Him their treasures.
Mary said, For whom are these?— and for what purpose? And what is the cause— that has called you to come from your country — to the Child with your treasures?
paragraphs.html: Mary said, For whom are these?— and for what purpose? And what is the cause— that has called you to come from your country — to the Child with your treasures?

paragraphs.text: They said, Your Son is a King, — and He binds crowns and is King of all — and great is His power over the world — and to His Kingdom shall all be obedient.
paragraphs.html: They said, Your Son is a King, — and He binds crowns and is King of all — and great is His power over the world — and to His Kingdom shall all be obedient.

paragraphs.text: At what time did this come to pass — that a lowly woman should bring forth a King? I who am in need and in want — how then could a king come forth from me?
paragraphs.html: <q>At what time did this come to pass — that a lowly woman should bring forth a King? I who am in need and in want — how then could a king come forth from me?</q>

paragraphs.text: In you alone has this come to pass — that a mighty King from you should appear — you in whom poverty shall be magnified — and to your Son shall crowns be made subject.
paragraphs.html: <q>In you alone has this come to pass — that a mighty King from you should appear — you in whom poverty shall be magnified — and to your Son shall crowns be made subject.</q>

paragraphs.text: Treasures of Kings I have not — riches have never fallen to my lot.— My house is lowly and my dwelling needy;— why then do you proclaim that my Son is King?
paragraphs.html: <q>Treasures of Kings I have not — riches have never fallen to my lot.— My house is lowly and my dwelling needy;— why then do you proclaim that my Son is King?</q>

paragraphs.text: Great treasure is in your Son, — and wealth that suffices to make all rich;— for the treasures of kings are impoverished — but He fails not nor can be measured.
paragraphs.html: <q>Great treasure is in your Son, — and wealth that suffices to make all rich;— for the treasures of kings are impoverished — but He fails not nor can be measured.</q>

paragraphs.text: Whether haply some other be for you — the King that is born, enquire concerning Him.— This is the son of a lowly woman — of one who is not meet to look on a King.
paragraphs.html: Whether haply some other be for you — the King that is born, enquire concerning Him.— This is the son of a lowly woman — of one who is not meet to look on a King.

paragraphs.text: Can it be that light should ever miss — the way whereon it has been sent? It was not darkness that summoned and led us — in light we walked, and your Son is King.
paragraphs.html: <q>Can it be that light should ever miss — the way whereon it has been sent? It was not darkness that summoned and led us — in light we walked, and your Son is King.</q>

paragraphs.text: Lo! You see a babe without speech — and the house of His mother empty and needy, — and of that which pertains to a king nought is in it:— how then in it is a king to be seen?
paragraphs.html: <q>Lo! You see a babe without speech — and the house of His mother empty and needy, — and of that which pertains to a king nought is in it:— how then in it is a king to be seen?</q>

paragraphs.text: Lo! We see that without speech and at rest — is the King, and lowly as you have said:— but again we see that the stars — in the highest He bids haste to proclaim Him.
paragraphs.html: Lo! We see that without speech and at rest — is the King, and lowly as you have said:— but again we see that the stars — in the highest He bids haste to proclaim Him.

paragraphs.text: It were meet, O men, that you should enquire — who is the King, and then adore him — lest haply your way has been mistaken — and another is the King that is born.
paragraphs.html: It were meet, O men, that you should enquire — who is the King, and then adore him — lest haply your way has been mistaken — and another is the King that is born.

paragraphs.text: It were meet, O maiden, that you should receive it — that we have learned that your Son is King — from the star of light that errs not — and plain is the way, and he has led us.
paragraphs.html: It were meet, O maiden, that you should receive it — that we have learned that your Son is King — from the star of light that errs not — and plain is the way, and he has led us.

paragraphs.text: The Child is a little one, and lo! He has not — the diadem of a king and of a throne — and what have you seen that you should pay honour to Him — as to a king, with your treasures?
paragraphs.html: <q>The Child is a little one, and lo! He has not — the diadem of a king and of a throne — and what have you seen that you should pay honour to Him — as to a king, with your treasures?</q>

paragraphs.text: A little one, because He willed it for quietness' sake — and meek now until He be revealed. — A time shall be for Him when all diadems — shall bow down and worship Him.
paragraphs.html: A little one, because He willed it for quietness' sake — and meek now until He be revealed. — A time shall be for Him when all diadems — shall bow down and worship Him.

paragraphs.text: Armies he has none — nor has my Son legions and troops:— in the poverty of His mother He dwells — why then King is He called by you?
paragraphs.html: <q>Armies he has none — nor has my Son legions and troops:— in the poverty of His mother He dwells — why then King is He called by you?</q>

paragraphs.text: The armies of your Son are above — they ride on high, and they flame — and one of them it was that came and summoned us — and all our country was dismayed.
paragraphs.html: <q>The armies of your Son are above — they ride on high, and they flame — and one of them it was that came and summoned us — and all our country was dismayed.</q>

paragraphs.text: The Child is a babe, and how is it possible — He should be King, unknown to the world?— And they that are mighty and of renown — how can a babe be their ruler?
paragraphs.html: <q>The Child is a babe, and how is it possible — He should be King, unknown to the world?— And they that are mighty and of renown — how can a babe be their ruler?</q>

paragraphs.text: Your babe is aged, O Virgin — and Ancient of Days and exalted above all and Adam beside Him is very babe — and in Him all created things are made new.
paragraphs.html: Your babe is aged, O Virgin — and Ancient of Days and exalted above all and Adam beside Him is very babe — and in Him all created things are made new.

paragraphs.text: It is very seemly that you should expound — all the mystery and explain it — who it is that reveals to you the mystery of my Son, — that He is a King in your region.
paragraphs.html: It is very seemly that you should expound — all the mystery and explain it — who it is that reveals to you the mystery of my Son, — that He is a King in your region.

paragraphs.text: It is likewise seemly for you to accept this — that unless the truth had led us we had not wandered hither from the ends of the earth — nor come for the sake of your Son.
paragraphs.html: <q>It is likewise seemly for you to accept this — that unless the truth had led us we had not wandered hither from the ends of the earth — nor come for the sake of your Son.</q>

paragraphs.text: All the mystery as it was wrought — among you there in your country — reveal to me now as friends.— Who was He that called you to come to me?
paragraphs.html: <q>All the mystery as it was wrought — among you there in your country — reveal to me now as friends.— Who was He that called you to come to me?</q>

paragraphs.text: A mighty Star appeared to us — that was glorious exceedingly above the stars — and our land by its fire was kindled — that this King had appeared it bore tidings to us.
paragraphs.html: <q>A mighty Star appeared to us — that was glorious exceedingly above the stars — and our land by its fire was kindled — that this King had appeared it bore tidings to us.</q>

paragraphs.text: Do not, I beseech you, speak of — these things in our land lest they rage — and the kings of the earth join together — against the Child in their envy.
paragraphs.html: Do not, I beseech you, speak of — these things in our land lest they rage — and the kings of the earth join together — against the Child in their envy.

paragraphs.text: Be not dismayed, O Virgin! — Your Son shall bring to nought all diadems, and set them underneath his heel — and they shall not subdue Him Whom they envy.
paragraphs.html: <q>Be not dismayed, O Virgin! — Your Son shall bring to nought all diadems, and set them underneath his heel — and they shall not subdue Him Whom they envy.</q>

paragraphs.text: Because of Herod I am afraid, — that unclean wolf, lest he assail me — and draw his sword and with it cut off — the sweet cluster before it be ripe.
paragraphs.html: <q>Because of Herod I am afraid, — that unclean wolf, lest he assail me — and draw his sword and with it cut off — the sweet cluster before it be ripe.</q>

paragraphs.text: Because of Herod fear not — for in the hands of your Son is his throne placed:— and as soon as He shall reign it shall be laid low — and his diadem shall fall on the earth beneath.
paragraphs.html: <q>Because of Herod fear not — for in the hands of your Son is his throne placed:— and as soon as He shall reign it shall be laid low — and his diadem shall fall on the earth beneath.</q>

paragraphs.text: A torrent of blood is Jerusalem, — wherein the excellent ones are slain;— and if she perceives Him she will assail Him.— In mystery speak, and noise it not abroad.
paragraphs.html: A torrent of blood is Jerusalem, — wherein the excellent ones are slain;— and if she perceives Him she will assail Him.— In mystery speak, and noise it not abroad.

paragraphs.text: All torrents, and likewise swords — by the hands of your Son shall be appeased — and the sword of Jerusalem shall be blunted — and shall not desire at all to kill.
paragraphs.html: <q>All torrents, and likewise swords — by the hands of your Son shall be appeased — and the sword of Jerusalem shall be blunted — and shall not desire at all to kill.</q>

paragraphs.text: The scribes of the priests of Jerusalem — pour forth blood and heed not.— They will arouse murderous strife — against me and against the Child; O Magi, be silent!
paragraphs.html: <q>The scribes of the priests of Jerusalem — pour forth blood and heed not.— They will arouse murderous strife — against me and against the Child; O Magi, be silent!</q>

paragraphs.text: The scribes and the priests will be unable — to hurt your son in their envy — for by Him their priesthood shall be dissolved — and their festivals brought to nought.
paragraphs.html: <q>The scribes and the priests will be unable — to hurt your son in their envy — for by Him their priesthood shall be dissolved — and their festivals brought to nought.</q>

paragraphs.text: A Watcher revealed to me, when I received — conception of the Babe, that my Son is a King;— that His diadem is from on high and is not dissolved — he declared to me even as you do.
paragraphs.html: A Watcher revealed to me, when I received — conception of the Babe, that my Son is a King;— that His diadem is from on high and is not dissolved — he declared to me even as you do.

paragraphs.text: The Watcher, therefore, of whom you have spoken — is he who came as a star — and was shown to us and brought us good tidings — that He is great and glorious above the stars.
paragraphs.html: <q>The Watcher, therefore, of whom you have spoken — is he who came as a star — and was shown to us and brought us good tidings — that He is great and glorious above the stars.</q>

paragraphs.text: That Angel declared to me — in his good tidings, when he appeared to me — that to His Kingdom no end shall be — and the mystery is kept and shall not be revealed.
paragraphs.html: <q>That Angel declared to me — in his good tidings, when he appeared to me — that to His Kingdom no end shall be — and the mystery is kept and shall not be revealed.</q>

paragraphs.text: The Star also declared again to us — that your Son is He that shall keep the diadem.— His aspect was something changed — and he was the Angel and made it not known to us.
paragraphs.html: The Star also declared again to us — that your Son is He that shall keep the diadem.— His aspect was something changed — and he was the Angel and made it not known to us.

paragraphs.text: Before me when the Watcher showed himself — he called Him his Lord before He was conceived — and as the Son of the Highest announced Him to me:— but where His Father is he made not known to me.
paragraphs.html: <q>Before me when the Watcher showed himself — he called Him his Lord before He was conceived — and as the Son of the Highest announced Him to me:— but where His Father is he made not known to me.</q>

paragraphs.text: Before us he proclaimed in the form of a star — that the Lord of the Highest is He Who is born — and over the stars of light your Son is ruler — and unless He commands they rise not.
paragraphs.html: Before us he proclaimed in the form of a star — that the Lord of the Highest is He Who is born — and over the stars of light your Son is ruler — and unless He commands they rise not.

paragraphs.text: In your presence, lo! There are revealed — other mysteries, that you may learn the truth — how in virginity I bare my Son, — and He is Son of God; go, proclaim Him!
paragraphs.html: <q>In your presence, lo! There are revealed — other mysteries, that you may learn the truth — how in virginity I bare my Son, — and He is Son of God; go, proclaim Him!</q>

paragraphs.text: In our presence the Star taught us— that His Birth is exalted above the world and above all beings is your Son, — and is Son of God according to your saying.
paragraphs.html: In our presence the Star taught us— that His Birth is exalted above the world and above all beings is your Son, — and is Son of God according to your saying.

paragraphs.text: The world on high and the world below bear witness to Him — all the Watchers and the stars — that He is Son of God and Lord. — Bear His fame to your lands!
paragraphs.html: The world on high and the world below bear witness to Him — all the Watchers and the stars — that He is Son of God and Lord. — Bear His fame to your lands!

paragraphs.text: All the world on high, in one star — has stirred up Persia and she has learned the truth — that your Son is Son of God — and to Him shall all peoples be subject.
paragraphs.html: <q>All the world on high, in one star — has stirred up Persia and she has learned the truth — that your Son is Son of God — and to Him shall all peoples be subject.</q>

paragraphs.text: Peace bear to your lands:— peace be multiplied in your borders!— As apostles of truth may you be believed— in all the way that you shall pass through.
paragraphs.html: <q>Peace bear to your lands:— peace be multiplied in your borders!— As apostles of truth may you be believed— in all the way that you shall pass through.</q>

paragraphs.text: The peace of your Son, it shall bear us — in tranquillity to our land, as it has led us hither — and when His power shall have grasped the worlds — may He visit our land and bless it!
paragraphs.html: The peace of your Son, it shall bear us — in tranquillity to our land, as it has led us hither — and when His power shall have grasped the worlds — may He visit our land and bless it!

paragraphs.text: May Persia rejoice in your glad tidings!— may Assyria exult in your coming — And when my Son's Kingdom shall arise — may He plant His standard in your country!
paragraphs.html: <q>May Persia rejoice in your glad tidings!— may Assyria exult in your coming — And when my Son's Kingdom shall arise — may He plant His standard in your country!</q>

paragraphs.text: Let the Church sing with rejoicing —Glory in the Birth of the Highest — by Whom the world above and the world below are illumined!— Blessed be He in Whose Birth all are made glad!
paragraphs.html: Let the Church sing with rejoicing —Glory in the Birth of the Highest — by Whom the world above and the world below are illumined!— Blessed be He in Whose Birth all are made glad!
